description: The pie chart displays percentage labels (e.g., EURO at 36.2%, AMRO at 24.9%) that do not match the actual visual slice sizes — AMRO's slice appears far larger than EURO's despite EURO being labeled as the biggest region. A reader glancing at the chart would form a completely different understanding of the regional distribution than what the numbers state, creating a significant and misleading contradiction between the visual encoding and the annotations.
